revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''臼井塚古墳'''（うすいづかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]百草にある、[[古墳時代]]後期([[7世紀]]前半)の[[古墳]]。 ==...'
revision.text:
'''臼井塚古墳'''（うすいづかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]百草にある、[[古墳時代]]後期([[7世紀]]前半)の[[古墳]]。
== 概要 == 昭和27年([[1952年]])に[[稲荷塚古墳]]と共に調査が行われた。[[稲荷塚古墳]]の西方100m付近に位置する。 調査時には既に墳丘がなかったため墳形や規模は不明である。築造時期は7世紀前半(古墳時代後期)と推測されている。埋葬者は[[稲荷塚古墳]]と同様に不明である。
石室は[[稲荷塚古墳]]と同じ羨道部・前室・玄室の三室からなっている[[横穴式石室]]である。石室は[[凝灰岩]]の切石を組み合わせて積み上げられた構造で、前室と玄室の壁は三味線の胴のようにカーブしており、胴張複室構造([[子宮]]の様な形）と呼ばれ精巧な技術で造られている。 玄室は長さ2.34m、最大幅2.15mの楕円形、前室は長さ1.2m、最大幅1.6m、羨道部は長さ1m程とされるが原形は不明である。
副葬品として鉄小刀が出土している。
[[稲荷塚古墳]]との築造時期の前後は不明である。現在石室は埋め戻されている。
元多摩市長の臼井氏の宅地内にある。
== 和田古墳群 == [[多摩川]]の南側にあたる[[大栗川]]（多摩川支流）両岸には「[[和田古墳群]]」と呼ばれる古墳群がある。[[稲荷塚古墳]]、臼井塚古墳もその一部である。ほとんどが[[古墳時代]]後期にあたる6世紀中頃から7世紀中頃にかけて造られた古墳である。
[[稲荷塚古墳]]、臼井塚古墳の近くには6世紀中頃から7世紀中頃までの100年間に渡り造られた[[塚原古墳群]]がある。[[江戸時代]]の[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]には「古墳が40～50基あった」とされている。しかし現在までに確認されているのは10基である。川原石を用いた横穴式石室を持つ円墳の古墳群であり、[[副葬品]]として[[大刀]]、[[鉄鏃]]、[[刀子]]、[[金環]]、[[はそう]]、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]などが発見されている。
他に[[庚申塚古墳]]、[[万蔵院台古墳群]]、[[中和田横穴墓群]]など6世紀中頃～7世紀中頃を中心に幾多の古墳があり、都内でも有数の古墳群のひとつである。
しかし現在はその古墳のほとんどは姿を消し、確認できる古墳は少数となっている。
== 参考文献 == * 多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世 * 多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳 1996
== 関連項目 == * [[稲荷塚古墳]] * [[北大谷古墳]] * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] * [[天文台構内古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うすいつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub|}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市]]

'''臼井塚古墳'''（うすいづかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]百草にある、[[古墳時代]]後期([[7世紀]]前半)の[[古墳]]。
== 概要 == 昭和27年([[1952年]])に[[稲荷塚古墳]]と共に調査が行われた。[[稲荷塚古墳]]の西方100m付近に位置する。 調査時には既に墳丘がなかったため墳形や規模は不明である。築造時期は7世紀前半(古墳時代後期)と推測されている。埋葬者は[[稲荷塚古墳]]と同様に不明である。
石室は羨道部・前室・玄室の三室からなっている[[横穴式石室]]である。石室は[[凝灰岩]]の切石を組み合わせて積み上げられた構造で、前室と玄室の壁は三味線の胴のようにカーブしており、胴張複室構造([[子宮]]の様な形）と呼ばれ精巧な技術で造られている。 玄室は長さ2.34m、最大幅2.15mの楕円形、前室は長さ1.2m、最大幅1.6m、羨道部は長さ1m程とされるが原形は不明である。
副葬品として鉄小刀が出土している。
[[稲荷塚古墳]]との築造時期の前後は不明である。現在石室は埋め戻されている。
元多摩市長の臼井氏の宅地内にある。

他に[[庚申塚古墳]]、[[万蔵院台古墳群]]、[[中和田横穴墓群]]など6世紀中頃～7世紀中頃を中心に幾多の古墳があり、都内でも有数の古墳群のひとつである。
しかし現在はその古墳のほとんどは姿を消し、確認できる古墳は少数となっている。

== 関連項目 == * [[稲荷塚古墳]] * [[北大谷古墳]] * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] * [[天文台構内古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:うすいつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub|}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市]]

{{no footnotes|date=2020年5月}} {{ページ番号|date=2020年5月}} '''臼井塚古墳'''（うすいづかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]百草にある、[[古墳時代]]後期（[[7世紀]]前半）の[[古墳]]。
== 概要 == [[1952年]]（[[昭和]]27年）に[[稲荷塚古墳]]とともに調査が行われた。稲荷塚古墳の西方100メートル付近に位置する。 調査時には既に墳丘がなかったため墳形や規模は不明である。築造時期は7世紀前半（古墳時代後期）と推測されている。埋葬者は稲荷塚古墳と同様に不明である。
石室は[[羨道]]部・前室・[[玄室]]の三室からなっている[[横穴式石室]]である。石室は[[凝灰岩]]の切石を組み合わせて積み上げられた構造で、前室と玄室の壁は[[三味線]]の胴のようにカーブしており、胴張複室構造（[[子宮]]のような形）と呼ばれ精巧な技術で造られている。 玄室は長さ2.34メートル、最大幅2.15メートルの[[楕円形]]、前室は長さ1.2メートル、最大幅1.6メートル、羨道部は長さ1メートルほどとされるが原形は不明である。
副葬品として鉄小刀が出土している。
稲荷塚古墳との築造時期の前後は不明である。現在石室は埋め戻されている。
元多摩市長の臼井氏の宅地内にある。
== 参考文献 == * 多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世 * 多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳 1996
== 関連項目 == * [[稲荷塚古墳]] * [[北大谷古墳]] * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] * [[天文台構内古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うすいつかこふん}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市の歴史]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Link complete]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) burial mound located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the burial mound itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} * {{ill|Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun|ja|武蔵府中熊野神社古墳}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=draft}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=draft}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=2020年5月}} {{ページ番号|date=2020年5月}} '''臼井塚古墳'''（うすいづかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]百草にある、[[古墳時代]]後期（[[7世紀]]前半）の[[古墳]]。
== 概要 == [[1952年]]（[[昭和]]27年）に[[稲荷塚古墳]]とともに調査が行われた。稲荷塚古墳の西方100メートル付近に位置する。 調査時には既に墳丘がなかったため墳形や規模は不明である。築造時期は7世紀前半（古墳時代後期）と推測されている。埋葬者は稲荷塚古墳と同様に不明である。
石室は[[羨道]]部・前室・[[玄室]]の三室からなっている[[横穴式石室]]である。石室は[[凝灰岩]]の切石を組み合わせて積み上げられた構造で、前室と玄室の壁は[[三味線]]の胴のようにカーブしており、胴張複室構造（[[子宮]]のような形）と呼ばれ精巧な技術で造られている。 玄室は長さ2.34メートル、最大幅2.15メートルの[[楕円形]]、前室は長さ1.2メートル、最大幅1.6メートル、羨道部は長さ1メートルほどとされるが原形は不明である。
副葬品として鉄小刀が出土している。
稲荷塚古墳との築造時期の前後は不明である。現在石室は埋め戻されている。
元多摩市長の臼井氏の宅地内にある。
== 参考文献 == * 多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世 * 多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳 1996
== 関連項目 == * [[稲荷塚古墳]] * [[北大谷古墳]] * [[武蔵府中熊野神社古墳]] * [[天文台構内古墳]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うすいつかこふん}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市の歴史]]

By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.

The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|臼井塚古墳|version=77514591|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.

The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

revision.text:
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|臼井塚古墳|version=77514591|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 5]]
}}

{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun]] * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tama|qid=Q213263}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11613223]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== Overview ==

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.

The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.

The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.

It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
