'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== Overview ==

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.

The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.

The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.

It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== Overview ==

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.

The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.

The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.

It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tama|WD=Q213263}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11613223]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early [[:en:7th century|7th century]]) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in [[:en:1952|1952]] (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tama|WD=Q213263}}}} {{no footnotes|date=May 2020}} {{page number|date=May 2020}}
'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}.
== Overview ==
The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=1952}} (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.
The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.
The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.
Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.
It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.
The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.
== References == * ''Tama City History: Archaeological, Ancient, and Medieval Materials'' (多摩市史 資料編1 考古 古代 中世) * ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kitaotani Kofun|lt=Kita-Otani Kofun|ja|北大谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Otani Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|en|Musashi Fuchū Kumano Jinja Kofun|WD=Q11546361|lt=Musashi Fuchu Kumano Shrine Kofun}} * {{ill|Observatory Site Kofun|ja|天文台構内古墳|1=Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Observatory Site Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|qq=}} * [[List of Kofun in Japan]]

The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.

It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

* ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:臼井塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Tama|WD=Q213263|1=Tama, Tokyo}}}}
{{no footnotes|date=May 2020}}
{{page number|date=May 2020}}

'''Usuizuka Kofun''' (臼井塚古墳) is a late [[Kofun period]] (early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}) Kofun located in [[Momogusa]], [[Tama City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.

== Overview ==

The Usuizuka Kofun was excavated in {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=1952|1=1952}} (Showa 27) along with the nearby {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}. It is situated approximately 100 meters west of the Inarizuka Kofun. By the time of the excavation, the Kofun itself was already destroyed, and therefore, the original shape and dimensions remain unknown. The kofun is presumed to have been constructed in the early 7th century (late Kofun period). Like the Inarizuka Kofun, the identity of the person buried in Usuizuka Kofun is also unknown.

The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber) composed of three sections: an {{ill|endō|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}} (passageway), a fore-chamber, and a {{ill|genkan|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genkan|qq=}} (main chamber). The structure is built with meticulously cut tuff stones, and the walls of the fore-chamber and main chamber curve inwards like the body of a shamisen, forming what is known as a {{ill|dōbari fukushitsu|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōbari fukushitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (womb-shaped complex). This reflects a high level of craftsmanship.

The main chamber measures 2.34 meters in length and 2.15 meters at its widest point, forming an elliptical shape. The fore-chamber is 1.2 meters long and 1.6 meters at its widest point, while the passageway is approximately 1 meter in length, though the original dimensions of these features remain unclear.

Artifacts recovered from the site include a small iron sword.

It is uncertain whether the Usuizuka Kofun was constructed before or after the Inarizuka Kofun. Currently, the burial chamber has been backfilled.

The kofun is located on private land, within the residence of the former mayor of Tama City, Mr. Usui.

== References ==

* ''Report on Tama City Archaeological Investigations No. 39: Tokyo Designated Historic Site Inarizuka Kofun'' (多摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告39 東京都指定史跡 稲荷塚古墳), 1996
